response: the concentration of the pollutant p(t, x) at time t (in days) and distance x (in kilometers) from the source is modeled by the partial differential equation d p/d t = d (d p/d x²) - v (d p/d x) - k p where d is the diffusion coefficient, v is the velocity of the river flow, and k is the decay rate of the pollutant given d = 0.05 km²/day, v = 1 km/day, and k = 0.1 day⁻¹ the solution to the partial differential equation is p(t, x) = e^{-x²} e^{-t} the optimal positions x1, x2, x3 for 3 facilities are 2 km, 4 km, and 6 km from the pollution source the total pollutant concentration at the town's water intake point x = 10 km is minimized by placing the facilities at 2 km, 4 km, and 6 km from the pollution source the letter g appears 14 times and the last word of the response is cancel
